topic: International Awards — Nobel, Booker, Oscar & Sports Honours

questions.text: Who was the first Asian to receive the Nobel Prize?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore received the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913, becoming the first Asian to win any Nobel Prize. His work Gitanjali was cited by the Swedish Academy.

questions.text: C. V. Raman received the Nobel Prize in Physics for:
questions.explanation: C. V. Raman received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930 for the Raman Effect — the inelastic scattering of photons by matter, which causes a change in light frequency. February 28 is National Science Day to commemorate this discovery.

questions.text: Which Indian writer won the Booker Prize for 'The God of Small Things'?
questions.explanation: Arundhati Roy won the Booker Prize in 1997 for her debut novel The God of Small Things, set in Kerala. It remains her only novel for 20 years until The Ministry of Utmost Happiness (2017).

questions.text: The Nobel Peace Prize 2014 was shared between:
questions.explanation: The Nobel Peace Prize 2014 was jointly awarded to Kailash Satyarthi (India) and Malala Yousafzai (Pakistan) — the only time an Indian and Pakistani shared a Nobel Prize, for their struggle against suppression of children and for child rights.

questions.text: The Nobel Prize in Economics was NOT part of Alfred Nobel's original will. When was it added?
questions.explanation: The Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences was established in 1968 by the Sveriges Riksbank (Sweden's central bank) in memory of Alfred Nobel. It is formally called 'Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel.'

questions.text: Salman Rushdie's 'Midnight's Children' has won the 'Booker of Bookers' how many times?
questions.explanation: Midnight's Children won the Booker Prize in 1981 and was then voted 'Booker of Bookers' (best Booker novel of all time) twice — in 1993 (25th anniversary) and again in 2008 (40th anniversary).

questions.text: Which Indian film song won the Oscar for Best Original Song in 2023?
questions.explanation: Naatu Naatu from the Telugu film RRR (directed by S. S. Rajamouli) won the Oscar for Best Original Song in 2023. It was composed by M. M. Keeravani — the first Indian song to win an Oscar.

questions.text: The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded by which institution?
questions.explanation: The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded by the Norwegian Nobel Committee in Oslo, Norway — the only Nobel Prize not awarded in Sweden. All other Nobel Prizes are awarded by Swedish institutions in Stockholm.

questions.text: Who was the first Indian to win an Oscar?
questions.explanation: Bhanu Athaiya won the Oscar for Best Costume Design in 1983 for the film Gandhi (directed by Richard Attenborough). She was the first Indian to win a competitive Oscar. Satyajit Ray received an honorary Oscar in 1992.

questions.text: The Ramon Magsaysay Award is often called the 'Asian Nobel Prize'. It is based in which city?
questions.explanation: The Ramon Magsaysay Award is based in Manila, Philippines, established in 1957 in memory of Ramon Magsaysay, the third President of the Philippines. It recognises greatness of spirit shown in service to the peoples of Asia.

questions.text: The Pulitzer Prize is primarily awarded for excellence in:
questions.explanation: The Pulitzer Prize is awarded for achievements in newspaper, magazine and online journalism, literature, and musical composition in the United States. It was established in 1917 and is administered by Columbia University.

questions.text: The Fields Medal is given for achievement in which field?
questions.explanation: The Fields Medal is the most prestigious award in Mathematics, often called the 'Nobel of Mathematics.' It is awarded every 4 years to mathematicians under 40 years of age. There is no Nobel Prize for mathematics.

questions.text: Amartya Sen received the Nobel Prize in Economics in 1998 for his work on:
questions.explanation: Amartya Sen received the Nobel Prize in Economics in 1998 for his contributions to welfare economics — particularly his work on poverty, famines, and social choice theory. His capabilities approach influenced global development policy.

questions.text: A. R. Rahman won how many Academy Awards (Oscars) for Slumdog Millionaire?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman won two Oscars for Slumdog Millionaire (2009): Best Original Score and Best Original Song (Jai Ho, shared with Gulzar). This made him the first Indian composer to win an Oscar for original score.

questions.text: Which novel by Aravind Adiga won the Booker Prize in 2008?
questions.explanation: Aravind Adiga's debut novel The White Tiger won the Booker Prize in 2008. The novel narrates the story of Balram Halwai, a self-made Indian entrepreneur from the lower caste, exploring India's class divide.

questions.text: The Turing Award is often called the 'Nobel Prize of Computing'. It is given by:
questions.explanation: The ACM A. M. Turing Award, given by the Association for Computing Machinery (ACM), is considered the Nobel Prize of computing. It is named after Alan Turing, the British mathematician who pioneered computer science.
